Home / Alternatives to ClickUp / ClickUp vs Vikunja

ClickUp vs Vikunja

A side-by-side look at ClickUp (the paid SaaS) and Vikunja (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

ClickUp Vikunja
Tagline One-app-to-replace-them-all workspace for tasks, docs and goals. Open source task manager with Kanban, lists and Gantt.
License Proprietary SaaS AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free Forever; Unlimited from $7/user/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Windows, macOS, Linux
Mobile apps Official apps typically available Android
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Individuals and small teams wanting multi-view task management.

Vikunja strengths

  • Multi-view — list, kanban, gantt, calendar.
  • Single Go binary for self-host.
  • CalDAV support.

Vikunja weaknesses

  • iOS app is community-maintained.
  • Smaller ecosystem.
  • Team collaboration features still maturing.

What's the catch with ClickUp?

  • Feature overload — steep learning curve for new users.
  • Performance can slow on large workspaces.
  • Frequent UI changes disrupt muscle memory.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to ClickUp: see ClickUp alternatives, or learn more about Vikunja on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
